channel.title: Context Engineering Daily - Research Papers
channel.description: Latest Research Papers news in Context Engineering

item.title: When Reasoning Narrows the Move: Diversity Collapse in LLM Game Play
item.description: arXiv:2607.19523v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Supervised fine-tuning (SFT) is widely used to adapt large language models to downstream tasks, but its effect on behavioral diversity in sequential decision-making remains under-explored. We study this question in a controlled suite of deterministic ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, arxiv, fine-tuning, large language model, study | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Scaling Laws for Hypernetwork-Based Knowledge Injection in Large Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2607.19604v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Injecting factual knowledge into large language models (LLMs) reliably and at scale remains an open challenge. Hypernetworks provide a promising solution to large-scale knowledge injection. Although hypernetworks are typically applied for test-time ad...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, example, arxiv, fine-tuning, large language model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: FineServe: A Fine-Grained Dataset and Characterization of Global LLM Serving Workloads
item.description: arXiv:2607.19349v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly deployed as always-on online services, making efficient LLM serving a critical systems challenge. Achieving low latency and high throughput under volatile demand requires deep understanding of real-world s...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, arxiv, large language model, analysis, platform | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Hybrid LSTM-Graph Neural Framework for Robust Financial Fraud Detection and Adversarial Resilience
item.description: arXiv:2607.19350v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Financial institutions face significant challenges in detecting sophisticated money laundering patterns, such as smurfing and layering, due to extreme data imbalance (0.13% fraud rate) and evolving adversarial evasion tactics. This paper proposes Frau...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, framework, arxiv, analysis, study | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Benchmarking Confidential GPU Inference on NVIDIA H100 under Intel TDX
item.description: arXiv:2607.19353v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Confidential computing is becoming a practical deployment requirement for AI inference workloads that process sensitive inputs or protect proprietary model assets. However, the performance cost of enabling confidential execution for GPU-accelerated la...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, arxiv, large language model, study, RAG | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: FormulaSPIN: Self-Play Fine-Tuning for Natural Language to Spreadsheet Formula Generation
item.description: arXiv:2607.19354v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Spreadsheet applications are used by hundreds of millions worldwide, yet writing formulas remains a significant barrier. Existing approaches rely on static supervised data, which quickly saturates on limited annotations. In this paper, we introduce FO...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, example, framework, arxiv, fine-tuning | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Information Discernment in Large Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2607.19355v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: LLMs are increasingly used with external knowledge sources like the internet. Do they weigh information appropriately -- updating more for reliable sources (source discernment) and more when claims bring priors closer to the truth (truth discernment)?...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, release, framework, arxiv, large language model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Bayesian Wind Tunnels for Model Selection
item.description: arXiv:2607.19379v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Prior work has shown that transformers can perform exact Bayesian filtering within a fixed hypothesis class. Can they also perform Bayesian model selection -- identifying the correct hypothesis class from data? We introduce model-selection Bayesia...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, transformer, LLM, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: CruiseBench: A Real-Flight-Aligned N-CMAPSS Benchmark for Engine RUL Prediction
item.description: arXiv:2607.19380v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Remaining useful life (RUL) prediction estimates how long an engine can continue safe operation and is central to maintenance planning. N-CMAPSS extends C-MAPSS by simulating run-to-failure aero-engine trajectories using recorded real-flight profiles ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, arxiv, RAG, paper, experiment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Challenges of Explainability in Continual Learning for Time Series Forecasting
item.description: arXiv:2607.19382v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Deep learning models have shown strong potential for time series forecasting, yet their deployment in real-world environmental monitoring remains challenging due to non-stationary dynamics and limited explainability. In this work, we investigate expla...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, augmented, attention, framework, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: STN-TGAT: Top-K Portfolio Construction via Prior-Guided Graph Attention with Learnable Soft-Threshold Sparsification
item.description: arXiv:2607.19385v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: This paper tackles the problem of stock ranking and portfolio construction under realistic investment settings by jointly modeling temporal dynamics and cross-sectional dependencies. We propose the Soft-Threshold NMI-prior Transformer Graph Attention ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> transformer, model, attention, framework, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: On the Computational Complexity of Structural Generalization
item.description: arXiv:2607.19573v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Structural generalization has been measured repeatedly by several benchmarks, yet it has never been formally defined. We give a definition that translates the two premises (compositional structure and unbounded generalization) into mathematical langua...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, transformer, paper | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Task Competence Is Not Instruction Following: Evaluating Instruction-Conflicting Behavior in Small Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2607.19608v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Instruction tuning is meant to make language models follow user requests, yet it is unclear whether small models comply when an instruction conflicts with their usual task behavior. We study this across three tasks - multiple-choice question answering...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, study, model, instruction | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Lightweight Person-Place Relation Extraction from Historical Newspapers with Dependency Graphs and Proximity Features
item.description: arXiv:2607.19718v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The HIPE-2026 shared task introduces person-place relation extraction from multilingual historical newspapers as a new evaluation track, classifying the at and isAt relations between pre-annotated person and location mentions in English, French, and G...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> attention, arxiv, model, paper | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: SUM: Unified Geometric Surgery on Spatio-Temporal Adaptation Vectors for Federated Class Incremental Learning
item.description: arXiv:2607.19384v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Real-world intelligent systems often require both distributed collaboration across data-isolated clients and continual adaptation to evolving tasks. This setting naturally gives rise to Federated Class Incremental Learning (FCIL), which combines Feder...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> vector, framework, arxiv, memory, vision | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Building Fast, Evaluating Slow: Pipeline Choices Dominate Autointerpretability Score Variance
item.description: arXiv:2607.19386v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Cross-paper comparison of sparse autoencoder (SAE) interpretability often relies on autointerpretability scores. In this evaluation pipeline, a language model (LM) explains each feature, and another LM scores the explanation. For these comparisons to ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> research, model, arxiv, tool, paper | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Scale-Aware Learning of Chaotic Dynamics on Unstructured Meshes via Binned Spectral Losses
item.description: arXiv:2607.19387v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Surrogate modeling for high-dimensional nonlinear dynamical systems that exhibit chaos requires mechanisms that preserve not only pointwise accuracy but also the scale-dependent structure of physical fields. Bandwise spectral power losses, such as the...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, arxiv, study, vision, alignment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Neural Operator Surrogates for Two-Dimensional Neutron Flux Estimation
item.description: arXiv:2607.19388v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: This work extends our one-dimensional single-sweep neural-operator studies to two dimensions. We consider one-group transport with isotropic scattering. As in the one-dimensional work, we use Fourier neural operators (FNOs) to approximate the high-fid...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, study, RAG | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Multi-Mask Diffusion Language Models for Few-Step Generation
item.description: arXiv:2607.19686v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Masked diffusion models (MDMs) are a promising family of language generators, but achieving high-quality few-step generation remains challenging. In MDMs, all forward trajectories collapse to a single fully masked state, leaving no terminal entropy fo...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> experiment, arxiv, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Stochastic Primal-Dual Decoding for Multiobjective Generative Recommender Systems
item.description: arXiv:2607.19357v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Recent advances in recommender systems (RS) have shown substantial performance gains through generative modelling. In practice, recommendation often involves constructing slates -- ordered lists of items -- that must satisfy multiple objectives beyond...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> experiment, arxiv, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)
